rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PHYLLIS M. MOSSMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Many of the receipts that were received in prior accounting were no longer received during this accounting. Petitioner alleges that she reported in her previous accounting that Conservatee's income from New York Life, retirement, and social security benefits were re-routed to Conservatee's trust accounts for ease of management and to minimize fees and expenses of these dual administrations (see Supplemental filed on 1/25/19). 2) $19,000 was distributed to the trust, rather than directly for conservatee's expenses, without prior court authority. Petitioner alleges that the average cost for Conservatee's monthly care is ~$10,000, and the monthly income received in the trust is ~$7,000, and the conservatorship funds were transferred to the trust account, as payments are automatically taken out from the trust accounts.
